title: In response to Whose soul is in Unit 00
display_title: In response to Whose soul is in Unit 00
wikitext:
[]: In response to: "Whose soul is in Unit 00?"             Quote Post by DarkJesus » Sun Dec 04, 2005 5:15 pm I thought of this a while ago. I have seen many people asking the question "Whose soul is in unit 00 and how does Rei sync with it"? Well, I have a theory (one that may have already been adressed). In the episode with Kaworu, he uses Unit 02 to attack 01. When asked how he is able to do this, he says that he is able to synchronise with any Eva that has no pilot or one where the pilot is distanced from their Eva (I forgot his actual words). Anyway, at another point in the series, Kaworu says to Rei that she is the same as him (Angels in human bodies). So wouldn't this mean that Rei is also able to sync with an Eva with no pilot (Unit 00)? I hope you understand what I am trying to say.

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Sun Dec 04, 2005 5:38 pm The events during the cross-sync tests in ep #14 clearly show that it's Rei I's soul inside Zerogouki.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sun Dec 04, 2005 5:53 pm Space Penis wrote: The events during the cross-sync tests in ep #14 clearly show that it's Rei I's soul inside Zerogouki.

[]: Re: In response to the question, "Whose soul is in unit             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Dec 04, 2005 6:26 pm DarkJesus wrote: In the episode with Kaworu, he uses Unit 02 to attack 01. When asked how he is able to do this, he says that he is able to synchronise with any Eva that has no pilot or one where the pilot is distanced from their Eva (I forgot his actual words).

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sun Dec 04, 2005 6:27 pm Rei I is IN Unit-00, but as to whether her soul or some piece of Rei's is there, it's a matter of debate. What you mention is interesting. It may be that Unit-00 is soulless.

[]:             Quote Post by Eternal Yamcha » Mon Dec 05, 2005 3:04 am As far as I can tell... The only real defense for DarkJesus' statement is... Wasn't Rei able to synchronize with Unit 01? Atleast, to some degree. Wait wait, even if I am correct, that defense can be squandered (I think) by pointing out that Asuka and Shinji were both inside of Unit 02 and Asuka was still able to pilot it. Rei I is IN Unit-00, but as to whether her soul or some piece of Rei's is there, it's a matter of debate. What you mention is interesting. It may be that Unit-00 is soulless.

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Mon Dec 05, 2005 11:25 am Eternal Yamcha wrote: That gave me an interesting thought... Now bare with me... Would Naoko Akagi's action of killing Rei I be something that Gendo wanted?

[]:             Quote Post by Six Winged Angel » Tue Dec 06, 2005 2:04 pm what the......I thought that Ritsuko's mother was on unit 00, and that explains the berserk attacks, but Rei's existanse in it explain it too!

[]:             Quote Post by DarkJesus » Tue Dec 06, 2005 3:08 pm I don't understand why anyone would think Naoko is in unit 00. Rei has no motherly bond to her, which I though was necessary. If they wanted to just stick a soul in to make it work, why didn't they just not bother. Anyway, back to my initial point. OK, if Rei I being in unit 00 is official, then ignore this. But I thought/assumed that there was only one Rei soul and it is salvaged for the new bodies (Rei I-II and II-III). Wouldn't this mean that there would be no "spare" for use in unit 00? I know this seems a bit wierd, but reply if you understand.

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Tue Dec 06, 2005 3:16 pm I think the general consensus is that Lilith's soul is odd enough to allow it to be in more than one place at once.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Tue Dec 06, 2005 3:17 pm DarkJesus wrote: I don't understand why anyone would think Naoko is in unit 00. Rei has no motherly bond to her, which I though was necessary. If they wanted to just stick a soul in to make it work, why didn't they just not bother. Anyway, back to my initial point. OK, if Rei I being in unit 00 is official, then ignore this. But I thought/assumed that there was only one Rei soul and it is salvaged for the new bodies (Rei I-II and II-III). Wouldn't this mean that there would be no "spare" for use in unit 00? I know this seems a bit wierd, but reply if you understand.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Noodle » Thu Dec 08, 2005 8:45 am In order to create functional entry plugs that would allow the pilot to synchronize with the EVA unit, human brain patterns and a mothering instinct needed to be mapped. Naoko Akagi was used for Unit 00, Kyoko Zeppelin was used for Unit 02, and Yui for EVA 01. The AT-field that each EVA can generate comes from a human soul that it has previously absorbed. Notice that the women used in this experiment are all mothers, some better at it than others. It follows that Unit 00 has a tendency to go berserk. Naoko never liked Yui and she certainly disliked Rei. But the bad behavior exhibited by Unit 00 is also tied to the idea that Naoko was never a very good mother. Ritsuko hated her. Unit 00 does not nurture its pilot and it certainly does not protect it. Because Naoko was obsessed with Ikari and a bit jealous of her daughter, it follows that when Unit 00 goes berserk it usually tries to attack them. Or Rei. Or Ritsuko. Only fourteen-year-olds born after the Second Impact can operate Evangelion. Only fourteen-year-olds can give the humanoid monster a soul. The children must synchronize with the unit. They are motherless children. One reason why Unit 03 was unstable is because the entry plug was not given the imprint of a real mom. There was no one to nurture Touji, to keep him safe. The imprint belonged to Rei, who is already composed of two other imprints. Despite the many references to God, Neon Genesis Evangelion is very much a Goddess-Mother tale. There are lots of biblical references. Try not to look into them, this is a common trick of anime to appear deep, but really they have no idea what they are aiming at.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Thu Dec 08, 2005 8:59 am ^ Welcome to the forum! The theory that Naoko' soul somehow ended up in Unit-00 has been thoroughly debunked for years now. There is no real evidence at all to support the notion, and plenty of evidence that contradicts it.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Dec 08, 2005 5:20 pm I gave an Agatha Christie style elimination lineup refute to the Naoko theory on Trivialbeing trivialbeing a few moons back, along with the standard floating head evidence. Shin-Seiki also presented another interesting refutation here . The Naoko theory seems to have been an attempt to explain the occupant in Unit-00 using the "mothers" theory. It was a speculative theory, but based on extrapolation rather than direct observation, which is always a problem.

[]:             Quote Post by Eternal Yamcha » Fri Dec 09, 2005 4:11 am Only fourteen-year-olds born after the Second Impact can operate Evangelion. Only fourteen-year-olds can give the humanoid monster a soul.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Dec 09, 2005 1:44 pm Eternal Yamcha wrote: Asuka... Exactly how old is she in the series? I keep on saying 13, given that her birthdate is (as far as I know) 12/04/2001 (happy belated birthday to her), which would put her at 13 for the majority of 2015 and thus, the majority of the series (assuming that the series does end in 2016

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Fri Dec 09, 2005 2:41 pm Im not with on this one tines. I think its Tojis mom in the eva, like most pilots. Why else would they take the time to allude he dosn't have one. The mentions its him his dad, grandfather and sister at home.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Dec 09, 2005 3:01 pm Rei-in-03 is, as I suggested, a mis -interpretation of the elements in that scene - the proximity or Rei-as-dummy-system (which foreshadows another bit of ep17) and Ritsuko's comments about fixing the core.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Fri Dec 09, 2005 3:24 pm My bad sorry :oops:

[]:             Quote Post by Shwiggie » Fri Dec 09, 2005 4:51 pm One thing I think has been largely ignored is that one's "soul" does not necessarily mean one's "spirit". Some thinkers might presume the personality or mind to consitute the soul...copies of which could conceivably be made with the equipment and know-how to do so. This has been a universally accepted assumption in Evangelion for as long as I can remember. This explains how Rei remembers some things, yet she asserts that she is her own person and not in fact a doll. And given the "this is the me that you perceive" notions prevalent in the series, this image of Rei I could be a copy thereof that Rei II perceived while in the plug and saw in that manner. Rei is Rei...but I would think it's a matter of differentiation in her perception within that environment.
